issues.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer is not closed in a try-with-resources, leaking resources on exception
evidence: In handleSemanticFieldExplore (line ~479): `SemanticFieldExplorer explorer = new SemanticFieldExplorer(this.executor); ... ExplorationResult result = explorer.exploreByPattern(...); explorer.close();` — if exploreByPattern() throws, close() is never called
Same pattern repeated in handleSemanticField (~line 711) and handleSemanticFieldExamples (~line 789)
SemanticFieldExplorer implements AutoCloseable, so try-with-resources is the intended usage pattern
issues.suggestion: Replace all three usages with try-with-resources: `try (SemanticFieldExplorer explorer = new SemanticFieldExplorer(this.executor)) { ... }`

issues.summary: Integer.parseInt/Double.parseDouble on user query params without NumberFormatException handling — returns 500 instead of 400
evidence: handleSemanticFieldExplore (~line 474): `Integer.parseInt(params.getOrDefault("top", "15"))` — if user passes `top=abc`, throws NumberFormatException which propagates as HTTP 500
Same issue at lines ~475, 476, 477, 610, 611, 612, 708, 709, 787, 818 — all numeric params in all handlers are unguarded
The outer catch in the context lambda catches Exception (logs it and sends 500), so the user gets a 500 with an opaque error instead of a 400 with a useful message
issues.suggestion: Wrap numeric param parsing in a helper that catches NumberFormatException and calls `HttpApiUtils.sendError(exchange, 400, "Invalid parameter value: ...")`, or use a safe parseInt utility with a default

issues.summary: extractCollocateFromPattern() is a dead stub that always returns "unknown" regardless of input
evidence: extractCollocateFromPattern (line ~164): method body has `int pos2Index = findLabelPosition(bcqlPattern, 2);` but then unconditionally `return "unknown";` — the computed pos2Index is never used
No callers appear to handle or detect this failure mode; any code relying on this returns a meaningless value
issues.suggestion: Either implement the method body to actually extract the collocate using findLabelPosition, or remove the method if it is unused

issues.summary: getCorpusSize() method in BlackLabQueryExecutor has broken indentation, appearing at the wrong nesting level
evidence: After buildAndRankCollocates, the method `public long getCorpusSize() throws IOException { return 0L; }` appears with 8-space indentation directly following the closing brace of buildAndRankCollocates, visually suggesting it is nested inside another method
All other methods in the class use standard 4-space class-level indentation; this single method is a formatting outlier
issues.suggestion: Fix the indentation of getCorpusSize() to match the other class-level methods (4 spaces)

issues.summary: HttpApiUtils uses "UTF-8" string literals for charset while the rest of the codebase uses StandardCharsets.UTF_8
evidence: sendJsonResponse: `json.getBytes("UTF-8")` uses string literal
sendError: `json.getBytes("UTF-8")` uses string literal
parseQueryParams: `java.net.URLDecoder.decode(keyValue[0], "UTF-8")` uses string literal
By contrast, Main.java and BlackLabConllUIndexer.java consistently use `StandardCharsets.UTF_8` constants
issues.suggestion: Replace all `"UTF-8"` string literals in HttpApiUtils with `StandardCharsets.UTF_8` to match the rest of the codebase

issues.summary: WordSketchApiServer (the entire REST API layer) has zero unit or integration tests
evidence: No test file exists anywhere under src/test/ for WordSketchApiServer or HttpApiUtils
The server contains ~1000 lines of request routing, parameter parsing, response building, and business logic — all entirely untested
Error handling bugs like the unguarded parseInt and resource leaks are undetectable without tests
issues.suggestion: Add unit tests using a mock QueryExecutor: start the server on an ephemeral port, send HTTP requests with java.net.HttpURLConnection, assert response codes and JSON shape for key scenarios (missing params → 400, bad numeric param → 400, valid request → 200 with expected fields)

issues.summary: BlackLabSnippetParser has no tests despite containing complex, fragile XML/text parsing logic
evidence: BlackLabSnippetParser has ~15 static utility methods with non-trivial regex-based logic (trimToSentence, extractCollocateFromXmlByPosition, detokenize, findLabelPosition, etc.)
No test class exists for BlackLabSnippetParser
The dead stub extractCollocateFromPattern (always returns "unknown") would be caught immediately by even a single test
issues.suggestion: Add a BlackLabSnippetParserTest with pure unit tests over sample XML strings for each public/package-private method, especially findLabelPosition, extractCollocateFromXmlByPosition, trimToSentence, and detokenize

issues.summary: All integration tests require a hardcoded Windows index path (D:\corpora_philsci\bi) and are always skipped in any CI environment
evidence: DependencySketchTest: `private static final String INDEX_PATH = "D:\\corpora_philsci\\bi";` with `Assumptions.assumeTrue(indexDir.exists(), ...)`
BlackLabDummyTest and BlackLabBcqlConcordanceTest have the same pattern
Tests that do run only assert `assertNotNull(results)` — no actual content or behavior is verified
issues.suggestion: Build a small in-memory test index from bundled test-data/ CoNLL-U sentences (the project already has test-data/), run BlackLab indexing in @BeforeAll, and execute real queries against it — removing the Assumptions.assumeTrue guards

issues.summary: README documents /api/bcql as GET but the implementation is POST
evidence: README.md line 119: `GET /api/bcql`
WordSketchApiServer.java line ~229: `server.createContext("/api/bcql", ...)` delegates to handleBcqlQueryPost which explicitly calls `requireMethod(exchange, "POST")`
Server startup log also states: `POST /api/bcql - Execute BCQL query`
issues.suggestion: Change README.md line 119 from `GET /api/bcql` to `POST /api/bcql` and add a curl example showing the POST body format

issues.summary: Invalid numeric query parameters (e.g. top=abc) produce HTTP 500 instead of 400, violating API contract
evidence: handleSemanticFieldExplore calls `Integer.parseInt(params.getOrDefault("top", "15"))` without try-catch; a malformed value propagates as NumberFormatException caught by the outer `catch (Exception e)` which sends HTTP 500

Same issue in handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti (top, min_shared, min_logdice), handleSemanticField (min_logdice, max_per_noun), handleSemanticFieldExamples (max), handleConcordanceExamples (limit) — 10+ call sites
HTTP 500 implies a server-side fault; a user-supplied bad value should produce 400 Bad Request
issues.suggestion: Add a helper `parseIntParam(params, key, defaultVal)` that catches NumberFormatException and calls sendError(exchange, 400, ...) then returns false; use it consistently across all handlers

issues.summary: OPTIONS preflight is handled on POST endpoints but all GET endpoints return 405 for OPTIONS requests
evidence: handleVisualRadial and handleBcqlQueryPost both check `if ("OPTIONS".equalsIgnoreCase(method))` and call `HttpApiUtils.sendOptionsResponse(exchange, "POST")`
All 9 GET endpoint context handlers call `HttpApiUtils.requireMethod(exchange, "GET")` which returns 405 for any non-GET method including OPTIONS
CORS headers (Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *) are set on all JSON responses, but a browser making a cross-origin request with custom headers will send an OPTIONS preflight that gets rejected with 405
issues.suggestion: Update requireMethod() or add an OPTIONS check before the method guard in each GET context handler, or extend HttpApiUtils.requireMethod to automatically respond 204 with CORS headers for OPTIONS requests
